content:
Let 391=17·23.  In general 
 lcm(a,b)>lcm(a,391) 
exactly when there is a prime p for which 
max(v_p(a),v_p(b))>max(v_p(a),v_p(391)), 
and moreover all primes dividing 391 that b itself misses must be supplied by a.  

Thus b must  
1) contain at least one “new” prime p∉{17,23},  
2) contain exactly one of 17 or 23 (so that a can supply the other),  
and 3) be as small as possible.  

The smallest new prime is 2, and the smaller of {17,23} is 17, so the minimal b is  
 b=2·17=34.  
Indeed, with a=23 we get  
 lcm(23,34)=782>lcm(23,391)=391,  
so (23,34) is a pupusa.  No smaller b works.  

Answer: \boxed{34}

 lcm(a,b)>lcm(a,391)

Thus b must  
1) contain at least one “new” prime p∉{17,23},

The smallest new prime is 2, and the smaller of {17,23} is 17, so the minimal b is  
 b=2·17=34.

 lcm(23,34)=782>lcm(23,391)=391,
